title: juncus laeviusculus subsp. illawarrensis
intro: Juncus laeviusculus subsp. illawarrensis, commonly known as Illawarra rush or Illawarra juncus, is a subspecies of plant that belongs to the family Juncaceae and is native to the eastern coast of Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Juncus laeviusculus subsp. illawarrensis include its ability to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and reduce inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or added to topical creams and ointments for its medicinal properties.
